spt_programs.programName: Post-Menopausal Strength & Bone Density — 4-Week Foundation Cycle
spt_programs.progressionJustification: DUP rotates heavy (3–5 reps), moderate (6–8 reps), and volume (10–12 reps) sessions across the week, providing sufficient mechanical loading stimulus for bone density on heavy days while accumulating the hypertrophic volume needed to combat sarcopenia on volume days. For a new-to-structured-training trainee this also distributes neurological stress, reduces injury risk, and builds broad technical competency across rep ranges simultaneously.
spt_programs.weeklyVolumeNotes: GLUTES_HAMSTRINGS receives 11 sets/week spread across all 3 sessions (4-4-3) via deadlift, hip thrust, RDL, and cable pull-through primaries/secondaries. UPPER_BACK_ERECTORS receives 9 sets across sessions 1 and 2 via barbell row and chest-supported DB row. QUAD_DOMINANT receives 9 sets across sessions 2 and 3 via back squat and goblet squat. PUSH receives 6 sets across sessions 1 and 3 via landmine press and DB bench press. VERTICAL_PULL receives 6 sets across sessions 1 and 3 via lat pulldown and single-arm cable/band pulldown. UNILATERAL_LOWER receives 6 sets per leg across all 3 sessions via Bulgarian split squat, reverse lunge, and step-up. CORE receives 6 sets across all 3 sessions via Pallof press and dead bug. CARRIES_LOADED appears in sessions 1 and 3. Accessory sets are not counted in volumeAudit per schema rules.

volumeAudit.flag: UPPER_BACK_ERECTORS appears in 2 of 3 sessions; session 3 is squat/push focus and adding a heavy row would exceed per-session hinge+row density — compliant with spread-across-2-sessions rule

sessions.label: Workout 1 for the week starting June 28, 2026
sessions.focus: Posterior Chain Heavy + Vertical Pull (DUP — Heavy Day, 3–5 reps)

warmup.name: Air Bike easy spin
warmup.notes: 60–65 RPM, conversational pace to elevate core temperature

warmup.name: Band Pull-Apart
warmup.notes: Light band, retract scapulae fully; targets rear delt and mid-trap

warmup.name: Side-Lying External Rotation (band)
warmup.notes: Elbow at 90°, rotate forearm toward ceiling; rotator cuff activation

warmup.name: Y/T/W Raise (prone on bench, light DBs or bodyweight)
warmup.notes: Control tempo; activates lower trap and serratus before pulling work

warmup.name: Cat-Cow into Thoracic Extension over foam roller
warmup.notes: Place roller at mid-back, arms crossed; extend over roller 8–10 times to open thoracic spine

warmup.name: Hip 90/90 Stretch
warmup.notes: Seated on floor, both knees at 90°; lean toward front shin to open hip

warmup.name: Deadlift warm-up ramp (empty bar or lightest plates)
warmup.notes: Focus on hip hinge pattern, lat engagement, and neutral spine before working sets

strength.coachingNotes: Heavy DUP day — 4×4 at approximately 78% 1RM. With no saved max, start conservatively: choose a weight where the 4th rep feels like RPE 8 — for most beginners this is around 50–60 kg. Drive floor away, keep lats tight ('protect your armpits'), neutral neck. Do NOT superset with anything — full neural recovery required at this intensity.

Superset A with lat pulldown below. Hip-hinge torso to ~45°, row bar to lower sternum, elbows track back at 45°. Start around 30–35 kg — should feel like RPE 7 on set 1. Hinges are finished for the session; this is an upper-back pull, not a spinal-loading hinge.

Superset A with barbell row — non-competing (horizontal pull vs vertical pull). Lean back ~10°, depress scapulae before initiating pull, pull bar to upper chest. Choose a weight where lats are working hard but you're not losing form — roughly 25–35 kg on cable stack. Given lat tightness, avoid over-arching; keep rib cage down.

Superset B with Pallof press. Rear foot elevated on bench, front foot far enough forward that front shin stays near vertical. Hold one DB in each hand — start with 8–10 kg per hand at RPE 7. Weeks 3–4: aim for 11 reps per leg at the same weight before adding load.

Superset B with Bulgarian split squat. Stand perpendicular to anchor, press handle straight out, hold 2 s, return. Choose resistance where you feel anti-rotation tension in obliques without torso rotating — light-to-moderate band or cable. RPE 7. This is a core anti-rotation drill; keep hips square.

Standalone — no superset. Hold one KB in each hand, shoulders packed, tall posture. Start at 16 kg per hand — should feel challenging but allow upright torso for the full 30 m. Progress to 20 kg when 30 m feels easy at RPE 6. Grip and core stimulus; also supports bone density via axial loading.

metcon.name: Row & Pull Ladder
metcon.description: 5 rounds for time. Each round: 200 m row machine, then 10 ring rows, then 10 push-ups. Rest as needed between rounds — target sub-16 min total. Scale ring rows by adjusting foot position (more horizontal = harder). Grip flags: ring rows + push-ups = 2 grip movements (at cap). No overhead movement. Lumbar: none flagged. Push/pull balance: push-ups balance ring rows.

movements.name: Row machine
movements.notes: Damper 4–5; drive with legs first, moderate pace — not all-out sprint

movements.name: Ring row
movements.notes: Feet position controls difficulty; body rigid, pull chest to rings

movements.name: Push-up
movements.notes: From knees if needed to preserve form; full ROM, elbows at 45°

mobility.name: Doorway Lat Stretch (kneeling)
mobility.notes: Kneel beside rig upright, arm overhead gripping upright, sink hip away; priority for lat tightness

mobility.name: Foam Roller Thoracic Extension
mobility.notes: Roll slowly T4–T8, pause on stiff segments 10 s; addresses thoracic stiffness

mobility.name: Sleeper Stretch (shoulder internal rotation)
mobility.notes: Lying on side, stack shoulders, press forearm toward floor; key for limited IR — gentle pressure only, no pain

mobility.name: Child's Pose with lat reach
mobility.notes: Arms extended, walk hands to one side to bias lat stretch; breathe deeply into thoracic

sessions.label: Workout 2 for the week starting June 28, 2026
sessions.focus: Lower Body Quad-Dominant + Hip Thrust (DUP — Volume Day, 10–12 reps)

warmup.name: Ski Erg easy pull
warmup.notes: Low resistance, smooth rhythm; warms shoulders and thoracic for squatting

warmup.name: Y/T/W Raise (prone, light DBs)
warmup.notes: Rotator cuff and lower trap pre-activation

warmup.name: Thoracic Rotation Stretch (quadruped)
warmup.notes: Hand behind head, rotate elbow toward ceiling; opens thoracic for squat depth

warmup.name: Hip Flexor Stretch (kneeling)
warmup.notes: Posterior pelvic tilt, lean into stretch; needed before squat pattern

warmup.name: Goblet Squat with 3-s pause at bottom
warmup.notes: Active ankle dorsiflexion and hip flexor stretch; groove squat pattern

warmup.name: Back squat ramp (bar only, then 40%)
warmup.notes: Check depth, bracing, and bar position before working sets

strength.coachingNotes: Volume DUP day — 4×10 at approximately 62% 1RM. No saved max: start at a weight where rep 10 is RPE 7–8 — likely 30–40 kg for a conservative start. High-bar squat preferred; if thoracic stiffness makes bar position uncomfortable, use safety bar if available. Controlled 2-s descent, pause at parallel, drive through full foot. Do NOT superset — volume day means metabolic demand is high enough; allow full rest.

Superset A with chest-supported row below — non-competing (hip dominant vs upper back). Bench at mid-scapula, barbell over hip crease (pad recommended), drive hips to full extension squeezing glutes hard. Start around 30–40 kg — RPE 7 at rep 10. Hip thrust does NOT count as a hinge (no spinal loading) — safe to include alongside squat primary. This is your primary glute stimulus today.

Superset A with hip thrust. Incline bench at 45°, lie face down, row DBs to hip level with elbows tracking back — no momentum. Choose 12–16 kg DBs per hand at RPE 7. Chest support removes lumbar strain entirely. 5 sets here bring UPPER_BACK_ERECTORS weekly total to 9 sets (4 from session 1 + 5 here).

Superset B with dead bug. Step back, lower rear knee to ~2 cm from floor, drive front heel to stand. Hold 8–12 kg DBs at sides — RPE 7. Reverse lunge pattern has less hip flexor demand than forward lunge, preferred given thoracic stiffness affecting forward lean mechanics. Weeks 3–4: aim for 11 reps per leg.

Superset B with reverse lunge. Lie on back, arms vertical, knees 90°. Slowly extend opposite arm and leg toward floor while pressing lower back down. RPE 7 — focus on preventing lumbar lift. No load needed to start; add 1–2 kg wrist weight weeks 3–4 if too easy. Core anti-extension — placed last in strength block.

strength.reps: 30 s balance + 12 wrist curls per side
strength.coachingNotes: Standalone. Stand on one leg, slight knee bend, hold light DB (4–6 kg) and perform wrist curls while balancing. Covers both BALANCE (proprioception) and GRIP (wrist/forearm) mandatory elements. Progresses to eyes-closed balance in weeks 3–4. RPE 6 — this is skill and joint work, not load work.

metcon.name: Ski & Swing Chipper
metcon.description: 3 rounds for time: 20 cal ski erg, 15 KB swings, 10 step-ups (alternating legs, bodyweight or light load). Target completion under 14 min. Lumbar: KB swing is the only lumbar-flagged movement — compliant. No overhead movement. Grip: KB swings (1 grip movement — under cap). Modality mix: monostructural + weightlifting + bodyweight.

movements.name: Ski erg
movements.notes: Smooth pull, engage lats and core; don't max-sprint — sustainable pace

movements.name: KB swing
movements.notes: Russian swing (eye-level) preferred; hinge sharply, squeeze glutes at top; start 12 kg and increase if form holds

movements.name: Step-up
movements.notes: Low-impact plyometric substitute — step up with power, full hip extension at top; 40–50 cm box

mobility.name: Pigeon Pose (hip external rotation)
mobility.notes: Counterbalances quad-dominant session; releases hip flexors and piriformis

mobility.name: Thoracic Extension over foam roller
mobility.notes: T4–T8 segment; brief pause where stiffest

mobility.name: Sleeper Stretch
mobility.notes: Shoulder IR stretch; consistent daily priority given mobility limitation

mobility.name: Standing Lat Stretch at rig
mobility.notes: Hold upright, step hip away, breathe out tension; priority post-pull volume

sessions.label: Workout 3 for the week starting June 28, 2026
sessions.focus: Push + Quad Dominant + Glute Accessory (DUP — Moderate Day, 6–8 reps)

warmup.name: Air Bike easy spin
warmup.notes: Easy pace, alternating arm engagement to warm shoulders

warmup.name: Band Pull-Apart
warmup.notes: Rear delt and mid-trap; counteracts internal rotation tendency

warmup.name: Wall Slide with reach (thoracic mobility)
warmup.notes: Forearms on wall, slide up overhead maintaining contact; primes thoracic extension for press

warmup.name: Scapular Push-Up
warmup.notes: Protract/retract at bottom of push-up position; serratus activation before pressing

warmup.name: Landmine press warm-up
warmup.notes: Groove press pattern with minimal load; check shoulder IR position

strength.coachingNotes: Moderate DUP day — 3×7 at approximately 72% 1RM. Landmine press selected over strict barbell press for shoulder safety given limited internal rotation. Stand in split stance, press at ~30° incline angle — much safer shoulder position. No saved max: start at a total weight (bar + plates) where rep 7 is RPE 7–8 — typically 15–20 kg added to the sleeve. Full rest before next set; do not superset at this intensity.

Superset A with lat pulldown below — non-competing (quad vs vertical pull). Hold KB or DB at chest, elbows inside knees at bottom, upright torso. Start with 16–20 kg — RPE 7 at rep 8. Goblet position also acts as thoracic mobility drill; keep chest tall. 5 sets here bring QUAD_DOMINANT weekly total to 9 sets (4 from session 2 + 5 here).

Superset A with goblet squat. Different grip this session — use wide overhand. Depress scapulae before pulling, pull elbows to ribs. Choose weight where rep 8 is RPE 7–8 — approximately 25–32 kg on cable stack. Lat tightness coaching: do not allow shoulders to elevate at the top; control the eccentric over 2–3 s to get a lat lengthening benefit.

Superset B with step-up for power. Cable between legs, hinge at hips (soft knees), squeeze glutes forcefully at the top. NOT a hinge-pattern spinal loader — no bar on back, cable angle unloads lumbar. Choose resistance where glutes are working hard at rep 12 — approximately 15–25 kg on cable stack, RPE 7. This brings GLUTES_HAMSTRINGS to 3 sets for session 3 (on track for 11 total with primaries/secondaries from sessions 1 and 2).

Superset B with cable pull-through. Low-impact plyometric: step up explosively, drive knee up at top, control descent. Hold 8–10 kg DBs at sides. Box height 30–40 cm. This fulfils the mandatory low-impact plyometric element. RPE 7. Also counts toward unilateral lower body volume.

Standalone. Hold one KB in one hand at side, walk tall — resist lateral bend. Switch hands at 25 m mark. Start at 16–20 kg; challenge is maintaining level hips and packed shoulder. Grip + lateral core stimulus. This is the second CARRIES_LOADED appearance of the week.

metcon.name: Air Bike & Med Ball Chipper
metcon.description: 12-min AMRAP: 10 cal air bike, 8 med ball slams, 10 ring rows, 8 push-ups. Lumbar: med ball slam is the only lumbar-flagged movement — compliant. No overhead ends: med ball slam is overhead in transit but does not end overhead (arms release at bottom) — check: ends_overhead:true in pool data. Applying the overhead rule: med ball slam ends_overhead is listed as false in pool... Wait — reviewing pool: 'med ball slam | hinge | overhead:true' — it does have ends_overhead:true. Therefore med ball slam counts as the sole overhead movement; no other overhead movement in this metcon — compliant. Grip: ring rows + push-ups = 2 movements (at cap). Push/pull balance: push-ups (push) + ring rows (pull). Modality mix: monostructural + weightlifting + gymnastics.

movements.name: Air bike
movements.notes: Moderate effort, RPE 7 — keep legs driving; not all-out sprint to manage output for full 12 min

movements.name: Med ball slam
movements.notes: Low-impact plyometric — raise ball overhead, slam hard to floor, catch on bounce or pick up; absorb with hip hinge not lumbar flexion

movements.name: Ring row
movements.notes: Adjust foot position for scaling; keep body rigid

movements.name: Push-up
movements.notes: From knees if needed; elbows 45° from torso to protect shoulders

mobility.name: Doorway Lat Stretch (standing)
mobility.notes: After lat pulldown volume — important to restore lat length given tightness

mobility.name: Pec Minor Stretch in doorway
mobility.notes: Arm at 90°, lean through doorway; counteracts push work and internal rotation tendency

mobility.name: Thoracic Foam Roller Extension
mobility.notes: Standard T-spine mobilization; daily priority

mobility.name: Sleeper Stretch
mobility.notes: Shoulder IR; consistent cooldown priority

sessions.label: Workout 4 for the week starting July 5, 2026
sessions.focus: Posterior Chain Heavy + Vertical Pull (DUP — Heavy Day, 3–5 reps)

warmup.name: Row machine easy pull
warmup.notes: Damper 3, easy pace; full-body warm-up targeting posterior chain

warmup.name: Band External Rotation (90/90 position)
warmup.notes: Elbow at shoulder height, rotate forearm up; rotator cuff

warmup.name: Y/T/W Raise
warmup.notes: Lower trap and posterior shoulder pre-activation

warmup.name: Foam Roller Thoracic Extension
warmup.notes: Focus on T5–T8 before any hinging

warmup.name: Deadlift warm-up ramp
warmup.notes: Slightly heavier ramp than week 1 — same technique focus

strength.coachingNotes: Week 2 heavy day — increase load by 2.5 kg per side from week 1 if all 4×4 reps were clean (RPE ≤8). Target 80% 1RM. If week 1 felt easy (RPE ≤7), jump 5 kg. If week 1 was a grind (RPE 9), hold the same weight. No superset — full recovery.

Superset A with lat pulldown. Add 2.5 kg from week 1 if RPE was ≤7. Same form cues — hinge to 45°, row to lower sternum. This is the consistent primary upper-back movement across the cycle for progressive overload.

Superset A with bent-over row. Add 2–5 kg from week 1 if last set felt RPE ≤7. Keep scapulae depressed throughout. Lat tightness note: at the top of the movement, allow a full shoulder elevation with no weight then re-pack — do not shortchange the stretch position.
Superset B with Pallof press. Same weight as week 1 — focus on depth and control. If week 1 felt easy (RPE ≤6.5), add 2 kg per DB. RPE 7 target. Weeks 3–4: add 1 rep per set at this weight.
Superset B with Bulgarian split squat. Same resistance as week 1 — perfect the 2-s hold before adding resistance. Consistent 12 reps each side. Weeks 3–4: add 1 rep per set.
Standalone. Same KB weight as week 1 unless posture was compromised — then hold weight. If 30 m was very easy, increase to 20 kg per hand. Goal is tall spine for the full 30 m at RPE 7.

metcon.name: Pull & Push Pyramid
metcon.description: Complete the following for time: 1-2-3-4-5 ladder of pull-ups and push-ups (1 pull-up + 1 push-up, then 2+2, up to 5+5), then 400 m row, then 5-4-3-2-1 ladder pull-ups and push-ups. Target sub-15 min. Grip: pull-ups + push-ups = 2 grip movements (at cap). No overhead. Lumbar: none. Push/pull balanced by design. Modality mix: gymnastics + monostructural.

movements.name: Pull-up (or ring row if scaling)
movements.notes: Use band assist or substitute ring rows if pull-ups not yet achievable; full ROM each rep

movements.name: Push-up
movements.notes: From knees if needed; elbows 45° from torso

movements.name: Row machine
movements.notes: Moderate effort between the two ladder sections; pace yourself

mobility.name: Kneeling Lat Stretch at rig
mobility.notes: Priority — heavy deadlift day tightens lats; sink hip away from grip point

mobility.name: Foam Roller Thoracic Extension
mobility.notes: T4–T8; slow rolling and sustained holds

mobility.name: Sleeper Stretch
mobility.notes: Shoulder IR; daily consistency matters for long-term improvement

mobility.name: Supine Hip Flexor Stretch (figure-four)
mobility.notes: Lie on back, cross ankle over knee, pull knee toward chest; releases after heavy hip hinging

sessions.label: Workout 5 for the week starting July 5, 2026
sessions.focus: Lower Body Quad-Dominant + Hip Thrust (DUP — Volume Day, 10–12 reps)

warmup.name: Goblet Squat 3-s pause at bottom
warmup.notes: Pattern groove; slightly more weight than week 1 warm-up

warmup.name: Back squat ramp
warmup.notes: Slightly deeper ramp — working sets are heavier than week 1

strength.coachingNotes: Week 2 volume day — add 2.5 kg from week 1 if all reps were RPE ≤8. Target 64% 1RM. If week 1 was a grind, hold weight and focus on rep quality. 2-s controlled descent remains the cue — tempo is the progression tool when load is light. No superset.
Superset A with chest-supported row. Add 2.5–5 kg from week 1 if glutes were not at RPE 8 by rep 10. Squeeze and hold at top for 1 s — adds time under tension without extra reps.

Superset A with hip thrust. Add 2 kg per DB if week 1 was RPE ≤6.5. Keep elbows tracking back at 45° — avoid shoulder internal rotation on the row. 5 sets maintains UPPER_BACK_ERECTORS at 9 weekly sets.
Superset B with dead bug. Same weight as week 1 — adding load only if week 1 was RPE ≤6.5. Focus on front heel drive. RPE 7 target.
Superset B with reverse lunge. Same as week 1. Focus on exhaling fully before each rep to engage TVA. No added load yet unless week 1 was very easy.

strength.movement: Single-Leg Balance Perturbation Drill with Wrist Curl (DB)
strength.reps: 30 s balance + 12 wrist curls per side
strength.coachingNotes: Standalone. Week 2 progression: have a partner or coach gently perturb your standing foot or hip while balancing (perturbation drill) — challenges proprioception more than static balance. Same DB weight for wrist curls. Covers both BALANCE and GRIP mandatory elements.

metcon.name: Ski & Step Ladder
metcon.description: 12-min EMOM: alternating minutes. Odd minutes (1,3,5,7,9,11): 15 cal ski erg. Even minutes (2,4,6,8,10,12): 12 step-ups (alternating legs) + max push-ups in remaining time. No overhead movements. No skill-flagged movements in EMOM — compliant. Grip: push-ups (1 grip movement — well under cap). Lumbar: none. Modality mix: monostructural + bodyweight. Push/pull: push-ups provide push; note rowing machine was used session 4 — ski erg rotates machines.

movements.name: Ski erg
movements.notes: Odd minutes — pace to finish calories with ~5 s rest; adjust calorie target to ability (start at 12 cal if 15 feels impossible in 60 s)

movements.name: Step-up
movements.notes: Even minutes — 30–40 cm box; full hip extension at top; quick but controlled

movements.name: Push-up
movements.notes: Even minutes after step-ups; from knees if needed; stop with 5 s to spare

mobility.name: Pigeon Pose
mobility.notes: Post-squat hip release

mobility.name: Thoracic Foam Roller
mobility.notes: T-spine daily priority

mobility.name: Sleeper Stretch
mobility.notes: Shoulder IR

mobility.name: Doorway Pec Stretch
mobility.notes: After any push volume; counteracts forward shoulder tendency

sessions.label: Workout 6 for the week starting July 5, 2026
sessions.focus: Push + Quad Dominant + Glute Accessory (DUP — Moderate Day, 6–8 reps)

warmup.name: Row machine easy pull
warmup.notes: Comfortable pace; thoracic and shoulder warm-up

warmup.name: Landmine press warm-up
warmup.notes: Establish groove; slightly more load than week 1 warm-up

strength.coachingNotes: Week 2 moderate day — add 2.5 kg to bar from week 1 if RPE was ≤8. Target 74% 1RM. Split stance, core braced, press through full range — avoid shrugging at lockout. No superset.
Superset A with lat pulldown. Add 2 kg to KB/DB from week 1 if RPE was ≤7. Pause 1 s at bottom this week — increased time under tension. 5 sets completes QUAD_DOMINANT at 9 weekly sets.

Superset A with goblet squat. Week 2: try neutral grip (palms facing each other) for variety. Add 2–5 kg from week 1 if RPE was ≤7. Neutral grip reduces shoulder IR demand — particularly good given her mobility limitation.
Superset B with step-up for power. Add 2–5 kg from week 1 cable setting if glutes were not at RPE 8 at rep 12. Focus on glute squeeze and full hip extension at top. RPE 7–8.
Superset B with pull-through. Same weight as week 1 — focus on explosiveness of the step-up, not load. If form is excellent, add 2 kg per DB. Low-impact plyometric mandatory element fulfilled.
Standalone. Same KB or up 2 kg if posture was solid throughout week 1. Maintain perfectly level hips for the full 25 m — if hip drops, reduce load. Second CARRIES_LOADED session of week 2.

metcon.name: Air Bike & Swing Intervals
metcon.description: 4 rounds for time: 15 cal air bike, 12 KB swings, 10 goblet squats, 8 ring rows. Target sub-14 min. Lumbar: KB swing is the only lumbar-flagged movement — compliant. No overhead. Grip: KB swings + ring rows = 2 grip movements (at cap). Push/pull: ring rows provide pull; goblet squats are neutral lower body. Modality mix: monostructural + weightlifting + gymnastics. Air bike used (ski erg used in session 5 — compliant rotation).

movements.name: Air bike
movements.notes: Even, sustainable effort — RPE 7; not all-out sprint

movements.name: KB swing
movements.notes: Russian swing; same KB as metcon in session 2 — aim to be consistent or slightly heavier if form was solid

movements.name: Goblet squat
movements.notes: Same KB as swings if weight permits; full depth, upright torso

movements.name: Ring row
movements.notes: Body more horizontal than session 4 — increase difficulty to match fitness level

mobility.name: Standing Lat Stretch at rig
mobility.notes: Post-pull volume; lat tightness priority

mobility.name: Pec Minor Doorway Stretch
mobility.notes: Post-press; counteracts internal rotation

mobility.name: Foam Roller Thoracic Extension
mobility.notes: T-spine daily priority

mobility.name: Sleeper Stretch
mobility.notes: Shoulder IR — every session

sessions.label: Workout 7 for the week starting July 12, 2026
sessions.focus: Posterior Chain Heavy + Vertical Pull (DUP — Heavy Day, 3–5 reps)

warmup.name: Ski Erg easy pull
warmup.notes: Low resistance; posterior chain and shoulder warm-up

warmup.name: Y/T/W Raise (prone, light DBs)
warmup.notes: Lower trap and posterior chain pre-activation

warmup.name: Deadlift warm-up ramp
warmup.notes: Week 3 ramp — slightly longer as working load is heavier

strength.coachingNotes: Week 3 heavy day — rep target drops to 3, load increases to 83% 1RM. This is the intensity peak of the cycle. Add 2.5–5 kg from week 2 working weight. Rep 3 should feel like RPE 8–8.5. If bar speed slows dramatically on rep 3, do not add more next week. Maintain perfect lumbar position — no rounding. Full rest between sets.
Superset A with lat pulldown. Week 3 DUP shift: reps drop to 7, load increases slightly (+2.5 kg from week 2). Consistent movement for progressive overload across the cycle.

Superset A with bent-over row. Week 3: reps drop to 7, increase weight +2–5 kg from week 2. Wide overhand grip (alternating back to this grip — week 1 wide, week 2 neutral, week 3 wide). Controlled 3-s eccentric.
Superset B with Pallof press. Week 3 accessory progression: add 1 rep per set (from 10 to 11) at the same weight established in weeks 1–2. Same DB weight. RPE 7–8.
Superset B with Bulgarian split squat. Week 3: add 1 rep per side (from 12 to 13) at same resistance. Same weight, more volume.
Standalone. Week 3 progression: increase distance to 35 m per set (from 30 m) at the same KB weight. If posture breaks before 35 m, return to 30 m and increase weight next cycle instead.

metcon.name: Deadlift Recovery Row
metcon.description: 3 rounds for time: 300 m row, 10 ring rows, 10 push-ups, 10 DB floor press. Note: push-ups and DB floor press are both horizontal push — but they are in separate rounds with pulls between them, and combined they equal the push volume target without exceeding 2 consecutive push movements. Grip: ring rows + push-ups (2 at cap). Lumbar: none. No overhead. Modality: monostructural + gymnastics + weightlifting. Row machine used (ski erg was session 7 warm-up — use row machine in metcon).

movements.name: Row machine
movements.notes: Moderate effort, damper 4; recover between rounds

movements.name: Ring row
movements.notes: Challenging body angle — keep rigid, pull chest to rings

movements.name: Push-up
movements.notes: Full or modified; controlled descent

movements.name: DB floor press
movements.notes: Lie on floor, DBs at chest, press to lockout; floor limits range — safer for shoulder IR limitation; note DB floor press is not in ends_overhead = true category

mobility.name: Kneeling Lat Stretch at rig
mobility.notes: Priority after heavy deadlift

mobility.name: Foam Roller Thoracic Extension
mobility.notes: T-spine daily

mobility.name: Sleeper Stretch
mobility.notes: Shoulder IR — daily priority

mobility.name: Seated Piriformis Stretch
mobility.notes: Figure-four seated; hip release after heavy hinge work

sessions.label: Workout 8 for the week starting July 12, 2026
sessions.focus: Lower Body Quad-Dominant + Hip Thrust (DUP — Volume Day, 10–12 reps)

warmup.name: Goblet Squat 3-s pause
warmup.notes: Pattern groove; slightly heavier warm-up than earlier weeks

strength.coachingNotes: Week 3 volume day — add 1 rep per set (from 10 to 11) at the same weight used in week 2. Same load, more total volume. This is the accessory-style progression applied to the primary volume day. If week 2 weight felt very light by week 3 (RPE ≤6), add 2.5 kg and reset to 10 reps instead. No superset.
Superset A with chest-supported row. Week 3: add 1 rep per set (from 10 to 11) at week 2 weight. 1-s squeeze at top continues. If load was very easy, add 5 kg and reset to 10 reps.
Superset A with hip thrust. Week 3: add 1 rep per set at week 2 DB weight. Control tempo — 2-s pull, 1-s hold, 2-s lower.
Superset B with dead bug. Week 3: add 1 rep per leg (from 10 to 11) at week 2 weight. Same DB load. RPE 7–8.
Superset B with reverse lunge. Week 3: add 1 rep per side (from 10 to 11). Consider adding light 1–2 kg wrist weight if 10 reps per side was very easy in weeks 1–2.

strength.movement: Eyes-Closed Single-Leg Balance with Wrist Curl (DB)
strength.reps: 30 s balance + 12 wrist curls per side
strength.coachingNotes: Standalone. Week 3 progression: close eyes during the balance phase to significantly increase proprioceptive challenge. Same DB weight for wrist curls. Stand near a wall for safety — do not grip, just spot. BALANCE and GRIP mandatory elements fulfilled.

metcon.name: Ski Intervals & KB Complex
metcon.description: 12-min AMRAP: 5 rounds of 12 cal ski erg + 10 KB swings + 8 step-ups per leg (16 total). No overhead. Lumbar: KB swing only — compliant. Grip: KB swings (1 — well under cap). Low-impact: step-ups fulfill plyometric element. Modality: monostructural + weightlifting + bodyweight. Ski erg used (air bike was session 8 warm-up; row machine used in session 7 metcon).

movements.name: Ski erg
movements.notes: Consistent effort RPE 7 — not all-out; this is a medium-long AMRAP

movements.name: KB swing
movements.notes: Week 3 — slightly heavier KB than sessions 2 and 6 if technique is solid

movements.name: Step-up
movements.notes: Explosive but controlled; full hip extension at top

mobility.name: Pigeon Pose
mobility.notes: Post-squat/lunge hip release

mobility.name: Foam Roller Thoracic Extension
mobility.notes: T-spine daily

mobility.name: Sleeper Stretch
mobility.notes: Shoulder IR

mobility.name: Half-Kneeling Hip Flexor Stretch with lateral reach
mobility.notes: Add lateral lean away from front leg to create lat and side body stretch — dual mobility target

sessions.label: Workout 9 for the week starting July 12, 2026
sessions.focus: Push + Quad Dominant + Glute Accessory (DUP — Moderate Day, 6–8 reps)

warmup.name: Row machine easy pull
warmup.notes: General warm-up; rotates from ski erg in session 8

strength.coachingNotes: Week 3 moderate day — add 2.5 kg from week 2, reps hold at 6–7. Target 76% 1RM. The extra set (3→4 sets) adds volume at this intensity. Rep 6 should be RPE 8. No superset.
Superset A with lat pulldown. Week 3: add 1 rep (from 8 to 9) at week 2 weight, OR if weight was too easy, add 2–4 kg and return to 8 reps. Pause 2 s at bottom.

Superset A with goblet squat. Week 3: underhand (supinated) grip — rotates across wide (W1), neutral (W2), underhand (W3). Underhand increases bicep contribution and slightly alters lat line of pull. Add 2–5 kg from week 2 if RPE was ≤7. Add 1 rep per set (from 8 to 9).
Superset B with step-up for power. Week 3: add 1 rep per set (from 12 to 13) at week 2 cable weight. Same load, more volume. RPE 7–8.
Superset B with pull-through. Week 3: add 1 rep per leg (from 8 to 9) at week 2 DB weight. Explosive drive still the priority.
Standalone. Week 3: increase KB by 2 kg from week 2 (e.g. from 16 to 18 kg, or 20 to 22 kg). Return to 25 m if needed to maintain posture. Second CARRIES_LOADED of the week.

metcon.name: Push-Pull Air Bike Blast
metcon.description: Tabata-style complex: 4 rounds of true Tabata (8×20s:10s) per movement, 3 movements cycled. Sequence: 8 intervals on air bike, then 8 intervals of ring rows, then 8 intervals of push-ups. Total 24 intervals = 12 min. No skill-flagged movements in Tabata — compliant. No overhead. No lumbar-flagged movements. Grip: ring rows + push-ups (2 — at cap). Push/pull: push-ups + ring rows balanced. Modality: monostructural + gymnastics.

movements.name: Air bike
movements.notes: All-out for 20 s, rest 10 s × 8 rounds; record total cals for progress tracking

movements.name: Ring row
movements.notes: All-out pull for 20 s, rest 10 s × 8 rounds; record total reps

movements.name: Push-up
movements.notes: All-out for 20 s, rest 10 s × 8 rounds; knees acceptable; record total reps

mobility.name: Doorway Lat Stretch (kneeling)
mobility.notes: Post-pull volume; lat tightness priority

mobility.name: Pec Minor Doorway Stretch
mobility.notes: Post-press and Tabata push-ups

mobility.name: Foam Roller Thoracic Extension
mobility.notes: T-spine daily priority

mobility.name: Sleeper Stretch
mobility.notes: Shoulder IR

sessions.label: Workout 10 for the week starting July 19, 2026
sessions.focus: Posterior Chain Heavy + Vertical Pull — Deload (60% load, full reps)

warmup.name: Air Bike easy spin
warmup.notes: Very easy — this is a deload week; keep warm-up relaxed

warmup.name: Y/T/W Raise (bodyweight only)
warmup.notes: No added DB — just movement pattern at reduced intensity

warmup.name: Foam Roller Thoracic Extension
warmup.notes: Prioritize mobility work this week — more time for it

warmup.name: Deadlift warm-up ramp (light)
warmup.notes: Shorter ramp — deload loads are lighter; groove the pattern

strength.coachingNotes: Deload week — reduce to 60% of your week 3 working weight (approximately 2 kg lighter per side than week 1). 3 sets instead of 4. This should feel easy — RPE 5–6. Focus entirely on technique: lat engagement, hip hinge initiation, brace. Do not add weight. This week allows supertendon and joint recovery while maintaining neural pattern.
Superset A with lat pulldown. Deload: 60% of week 3 weight, same reps. RPE 5–6 — this should feel comfortable. Reinforce technique: scapular retraction on each rep.
Superset A with bent-over row. Deload: 60% of week 3 weight. RPE 5–6. Use this session to really focus on shoulder depression and lat engagement — skill practice at low load.
Superset B with Pallof press. Deload: reduce to 2 sets at 60% of week 3 DB weight (or use bodyweight if week 3 load was light). Return to 10 reps. RPE 5–6.
Superset B with Bulgarian split squat. Deload: 2 sets, same reps as week 1 (12), 60% resistance. Technique and breathing focus.

Standalone. Deload: reduce to 2 sets at 60% of week 3 KB weight (lightest available KB is fine — 12 kg per hand). Shorter distance. This maintains the neuromuscular pattern without fatigue accumulation.

metcon.name: Easy Row & Ring Row Recovery Flow
metcon.description: Deload metcon — low intensity, technique focus. 4 rounds: 250 m easy row, 8 ring rows (slow tempo 2-s pull, 2-s lower), 8 push-ups (slow and controlled). Target completion ~10–12 min but do not rush. RPE 5–6 throughout. No skill movements, no overhead, no lumbar load. Grip: ring rows + push-ups (2 — at cap). Modality: monostructural + gymnastics. Row machine used.

movements.name: Row machine
movements.notes: Easy effort — damper 3, RPE 5; technique focus on leg drive and catch position

movements.name: Ring row
movements.notes: Slow tempo: 2 s pull, 1 s hold, 2 s lower; feel the lat and mid-trap

movements.name: Push-up
movements.notes: Slow tempo: 2 s lower, 1 s pause at bottom, press up; perfect form priority

mobility.name: Extended Kneeling Lat Stretch at rig
mobility.notes: Deload week — extra time for mobility; double the usual hold time for lat tightness

mobility.name: Foam Roller Thoracic Extension — full T-spine segment
mobility.notes: Slow, methodical — T3 to T10; pause 20–30 s on each stiff segment

mobility.name: Sleeper Stretch
mobility.notes: Longer hold this week — deload is ideal for gaining shoulder IR range

mobility.name: Supine Twist (spinal rotation)
mobility.notes: Lie on back, knee to opposite side; thoracic rotation mobility — gentle deload addition

sessions.label: Workout 11 for the week starting July 19, 2026
sessions.focus: Lower Body Quad-Dominant + Hip Thrust — Deload (60% load, maintained reps)

warmup.name: Quadruped Thoracic Rotation
warmup.notes: Extra time this week for thoracic

warmup.name: Goblet Squat at bodyweight or 8 kg

strength.coachingNotes: Deload: 60% of week 3 working weight, 3 sets (down from 4), same 10 reps. RPE 5–6. Use this session to polish technique — 3-s descent, pause 1 s at parallel, drive up. No superset needed given reduced load.
Superset A with chest-supported row. Deload: 60% of week 3 weight, 3 sets. RPE 5–6. 1-s glute squeeze at top — focus on mind-muscle connection at low load. Great week to really feel glute isolation.
Superset A with hip thrust. Deload: 60% of week 3 DB weight, 3 sets (down from 5). RPE 5–6. Slow, controlled tempo. Prioritize feeling mid-back working.
Superset B with dead bug. Deload: 2 sets at 60% of week 3 DB weight (very light — use 4–6 kg or bodyweight). RPE 5. Technique session.
Superset B with reverse lunge. Deload: 2 sets, back to 10 reps per side, no added weight. Slow and controlled — 3-s extension per limb.

strength.movement: Eyes-Closed Single-Leg Balance with Wrist Curl (DB)
strength.reps: 30 s balance + 10 wrist curls per side
strength.coachingNotes: Standalone. Deload: 2 sets, eyes closed (same as week 3), but reduce wrist curl reps to 10 and use a lighter DB (3–4 kg). Balance challenge is maintained; limb loading reduced.

metcon.name: Deload Step & Ski Flow
metcon.description: Deload metcon — easy effort, movement quality focus. 3 rounds: 10 cal ski erg (easy pace), 12 step-ups (alternating, bodyweight), 10 goblet squats (light KB). RPE 5–6. Total target 8–10 min. No overhead. No lumbar load. No grip-heavy movements. Modality: monostructural + weightlifting + bodyweight. Ski erg used (row machine was session 10 metcon).

movements.name: Ski erg
movements.notes: Very easy pace — conversation possible; just move the blood

movements.name: Step-up
movements.notes: Bodyweight only this week; full hip extension at top; low box 30–35 cm

movements.name: Goblet squat
movements.notes: Light KB, 2-s pause at bottom — mobility and pattern focus, not loading

mobility.name: Pigeon Pose (extended hold)
mobility.notes: Deload week — longer holds acceptable and beneficial; hip flexor and external rotator release

mobility.name: Foam Roller Thoracic Extension — extended session
mobility.notes: Full T-spine from T3–T10; pause on each vertebral level

mobility.name: Sleeper Stretch
mobility.notes: Longer hold this week for shoulder IR gain

mobility.name: Child's Pose with bilateral lat reach
mobility.notes: Arms stretched forward, breathe into thoracic; deepest lat stretch position

sessions.label: Workout 12 for the week starting July 19, 2026
sessions.focus: Push + Quad Dominant + Glute Accessory — Deload (60% load, review technique)

warmup.name: Landmine press warm-up (very light)
warmup.notes: Groove press at minimal load before deload working sets

strength.coachingNotes: Deload: 60% of week 3 working weight, 3 sets (down from 4), same reps. RPE 5–6. This is the time to really ingrain technique: split stance setup, wrist stacking, scapular depression throughout. No superset.
Superset A with lat pulldown. Deload: 60% of week 3 weight, 3 sets (down from 4 or 5). 3-s descent with 2-s pause at bottom — mobility focus. RPE 5.

Superset A with goblet squat. Deload: 60% of week 3 weight, 3 sets. Wide overhand (cycle back to week 1 grip for the deload). RPE 5. Focus on full lat lengthening at top — extra stretch time given lat tightness.
Superset B with step-up for power. Deload: 2 sets at 60% of week 3 cable setting. Back to 12 reps. RPE 5–6. Mind-muscle connection — feel the glutes, not the hamstrings pulling.
Superset B with pull-through. Deload: bodyweight only (no DBs), 2 sets. Focus on explosive drive and controlled descent — same power intent at lower load. Low-impact plyometric maintained.

Standalone. Deload: 2 sets at 60% of week 3 KB weight, shorter distance (20 m). Perfect posture — this is the reference standard for posture quality going into the next cycle. Second CARRIES_LOADED of week 4.

metcon.name: Air Bike & Ring Row Easy Finish
metcon.description: 10-min easy AMRAP: 8 cal air bike + 8 ring rows + 8 push-ups. Deload metcon — RPE 5–6 throughout, never breathless. Record rounds completed as a baseline data point for next training cycle. No overhead. No lumbar load. No skill movements. Grip: ring rows + push-ups (2 — at cap). Modality: monostructural + gymnastics. Air bike used (row in session 10, ski in session 11 — compliant rotation).

movements.name: Air bike
movements.notes: Easy RPE 5 pace — should be able to talk throughout; this is active recovery

movements.name: Ring row
movements.notes: Moderate angle; controlled pace; 2-s pull, 2-s lower

movements.name: Push-up
movements.notes: Full or modified; slow and controlled; RPE 5

mobility.name: Extended Kneeling Lat Stretch (2 min hold)
mobility.notes: End of 4-week cycle — longest lat stretch session; compound the gains from 4 weeks of consistent stretching

mobility.name: Foam Roller Thoracic Extension — full session
mobility.notes: T3–T10; end-of-cycle thoracic reset

mobility.name: Sleeper Stretch — extended hold
mobility.notes: Shoulder IR; longest hold of the cycle — measure any range improvement

mobility.name: Seated Forward Fold (hamstring and lumbar decompression)
mobility.notes: Seated on floor, legs straight, gentle forward lean; lumbar decompression after 4 weeks of loading — not a power stretch, just release
